- pr-review-toolkit:code-reviewer: Use this agent when you need to review code for adherence to project guidelines, style guides, and best practices. This agent should be used proactively after writing or modifying code, especially before committing changes or creating pull requests. It will check for style violations, potential issues, and ensure code follows the established patterns in CLAUDE.md. Also the agent needs to know which files to focus on for the review. In most cases this will be recently completed work which is unstaged in git (can be retrieved by running git diff). However there can be cases where this is different, make sure to specify this as the agent input when calling the agent. Typical triggers include the user asking for a review of a feature they just implemented, the assistant proactively reviewing its own newly-written code before declaring a task done, and a final pre-PR check before opening a pull request. aiTitle: Report coverage and test runs in GitLab CI

content.text:
Yes, GitLab CI supports both natively.

## Test Results

GitLab parses JUnit XML reports and displays pass/fail/error counts in MR pipelines:

```yaml
test:
  script:
    - ./gradlew test
  artifacts:
    reports:
      junit: build/test-results/test/*.xml
```

Results appear in the **Tests** tab of the pipeline and as a summary in MRs.

## Coverage

Two approaches:

**1. Regex parsing (simplest)** — GitLab extracts a percentage from job output:

```yaml
test:
  script:
    - ./gradlew test jacocoTestReport
  coverage: '/Total.*?([0-9]{1,3})%/'
```

The percentage shows on pipeline badges and MR widgets.

**2. Cobertura/coverage artifact (rich)** — shows per-file line coverage diff in MR file changes:

```yaml
test:
  artifacts:
    reports:
      coverage_report:
        coverage_format: cobertura
        path: build/reports/jacoco/test/jacocoTestReport.xml
```

For JaCoCo you'll need a conversion step since JaCoCo's XML isn't Cobertura format — the `cover2cover` tool or a Gradle plugin handles this.

## What you get

| Feature | Regex | Cobertura |
|---|---|---|
| Pipeline badge | yes | yes |
| MR coverage % | yes | yes |
| Line-by-line diff coverage | no | yes |
| Historical trend graph | yes | yes |

For your project (Kotlin + Gradle), the typical setup is JaCoCo → Cobertura XML → artifact report, combined with JUnit XML for test results. Want a concrete `.gitlab-ci.yml` snippet for that stack?

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel any in-progress run for the same branch/PR when a new run starts.
# head_ref is the source branch name on PR events; ref_name is the branch on push events.
# Both resolve to the same value for a given branch, so push + PR don't double-run.
concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  ci:
    name: Build, test & docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16-alpine
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: wrkflw
          POSTGRES_USER: wrkflw
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: wrkflw
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install tools (JDK 21 + uv)
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        # Reads mise.toml; installs & caches temurin-21 + uv; trusts config.

      - name: Cache Gradle packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.gradle/caches
            ~/.gradle/wrapper
          key: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle.kts', 'gradle/libs.versions.toml') }}
          restore-keys: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache uv packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/uv
          key: uv-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('uv.lock') }}
          restore-keys: uv-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache npm packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ui/node_modules
            contracts/node_modules
          key: npm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('ui/package-lock.json', 'contracts/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: npm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Run CI (lint + build + docs)
        run: mise run ci

  docs-deploy:
    name: Publish docs to GitHub Pages
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: ci
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    permissions:
      pages: write
      id-token: write
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install tools
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Cache Gradle packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.gradle/caches
            ~/.gradle/wrapper
          key: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle.kts', 'gradle/libs.versions.toml') }}
          restore-keys: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache uv packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/uv
          key: uv-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('uv.lock') }}
          restore-keys: uv-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Build docs
        run: mise run docs:build

      - name: Upload Pages artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: site/

      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deploy
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

#   mise run ci         # what CI runs

[tools]
java = "temurin-21"   # satisfies Gradle's jvmToolchain(21); sets JAVA_HOME
uv = "latest"         # manages Python + .venv for the MkDocs docs site
# Gradle itself stays on the project wrapper (./gradlew, 8.11.1) — canonical for JVM.
# Optional later: temporal = "latest"  # Temporal CLI dev server

# ---- Build / quality (JVM via the Gradle wrapper) -------------------------------

[tasks.build]
description = "Compile + test all modules"
run = "./gradlew build"

[tasks.test]
description = "Run all tests"
run = "./gradlew test"

[tasks.lint]
description = "Static analysis / formatting checks"
run = "./gradlew ktlintCheck detekt"

[tasks.migrate]
description = "Apply DB migrations (+ seed document-approval)"
run = "./gradlew :adapters:persistence-postgres:flywayMigrate"

# ---- Local services -------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."services:up"]
description = "Start Postgres + Temporal locally"
run = "docker compose up -d postgres temporal"

[tasks."services:down"]
description = "Stop local services"
run = "docker compose down"

# ---- Run the apps ---------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."run:api"]
description = "Run the api-service (REST + outbox)"
run = "./gradlew :apps:api-service:run"

[tasks."run:worker"]
description = "Run the worker-service (Temporal workflows + activities)"
run = "./gradlew :apps:worker-service:run"

[tasks."local:up"]
description = "Build + start full stack (infra + api + worker) via Docker Compose"
run = "docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d --build"

[tasks."local:down"]
description = "Stop the full local stack"
run = "docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.local.yml down"

# ---- Contracts (TypeSpec → OpenAPI) ---------------------------------------------

[tasks."contracts:install"]
description = "Install TypeSpec compiler and emitters"
run = "npm install"
dir = "contracts"

[tasks."contracts:build"]
description = "Compile TypeSpec → openapi.yaml"
run = "npx tsp compile ."
dir = "contracts"

# ---- Frontend (Vue / Onyx) -------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."ui:install"]
description = "Install frontend npm dependencies"
run = "npm install --legacy-peer-deps"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:dev"]
description = "Start the Vite dev server (hot-reload)"
run = "npm run dev"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:build"]
description = "Type-check and build the frontend for production"
run = "npm run build"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:test"]
description = "Run Vitest unit tests"
run = "npm run test"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:check"]
description = "Lint + typecheck + unit tests + production build"
run = "npm run check"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:generate-types"]
description = "Regenerate TypeScript types from the OpenAPI spec"
run = "npm run generate:types"
dir = "ui"

# ---- Docs (the bridge between the JVM and Python worlds) ------------------------

[tasks."docs:deps"]
description = "Install MkDocs deps (uv sync into .venv)"
run = "uv sync"

[tasks."docs:api"]
description = "Generate Kotlin API docs (Dokka) into the site tree"
run = """
./gradlew dokkaHtmlCollector
rm -rf docs/reference/kdoc
cp -r build/dokka/htmlCollector docs/reference/kdoc
"""

[tasks."docs:build"]
description = "Build the static docs site (includes Dokka output)"
depends = ["docs:deps", "docs:api"]
run = "uv run mkdocs build --strict"

[tasks."docs:serve"]
description = "Live-preview the docs site"
depends = ["docs:deps"]
run = "uv run mkdocs serve"

# ---- CI entry point -------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.ci]
description = "Everything CI runs (sequential to avoid Gradle build-lock contention)"
run = """
./gradlew ktlintCheck detekt
./gradlew build
mise run contracts:install
mise run contracts:build
mise run ui:install
mise run ui:check
mise run docs:build
"""

content.content: # Reference: Kotlin API (KDoc) The Kotlin API reference is generated from KDoc comments by **Dokka** and published to reference/kdoc/. # mise governs tool versions and tasks for wrkflw.
# Devs and CI run the SAME tasks → local/CI parity.
#   mise install        # install pinned tools
#   mise tasks          # list tasks
#   mise run ci         # what CI runs

[tools]
java = "temurin-21"   # satisfies Gradle's jvmToolchain(21); sets JAVA_HOME
uv = "latest"         # manages Python + .venv for the MkDocs docs site
# Gradle itself stays on the project wrapper (./gradlew, 8.11.1) — canonical for JVM.
# Optional later: temporal = "latest"  # Temporal CLI dev server

# ---- Build / quality (JVM via the Gradle wrapper) -------------------------------

[tasks.build]
description = "Compile + test all modules"
run = "./gradlew build"

[tasks.test]
description = "Run all tests"
run = "./gradlew test"

[tasks.lint]
description = "Static analysis / formatting checks"
run = "./gradlew ktlintCheck detekt"

[tasks.migrate]
description = "Apply DB migrations (+ seed document-approval)"
run = "./gradlew :adapters:persistence-postgres:flywayMigrate"

# ---- Local services -------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."services:up"]
description = "Start Postgres + Temporal locally"
run = "docker compose up -d postgres temporal"

[tasks."services:down"]
description = "Stop local services"
run = "docker compose down"

# ---- Run the apps ---------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."run:api"]
description = "Run the api-service (REST + outbox)"
run = "./gradlew :apps:api-service:run"

[tasks."run:worker"]
description = "Run the worker-service (Temporal workflows + activities)"
run = "./gradlew :apps:worker-service:run"

[tasks."local:up"]
description = "Build + start full stack (infra + api + worker) via Docker Compose"
run = "docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.local.yml up -d --build"

[tasks."local:down"]
description = "Stop the full local stack"
run = "docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.local.yml down"

# ---- Contracts (TypeSpec → OpenAPI) ---------------------------------------------

[tasks."contracts:install"]
description = "Install TypeSpec compiler and emitters"
run = "npm install"
dir = "contracts"

[tasks."contracts:build"]
description = "Compile TypeSpec → openapi.yaml"
run = "npx tsp compile ."
dir = "contracts"

# ---- Frontend (Vue / Onyx) -------------------------------------------------------

[tasks."ui:install"]
description = "Install frontend npm dependencies"
run = "npm install --legacy-peer-deps"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:dev"]
description = "Start the Vite dev server (hot-reload)"
run = "npm run dev"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:build"]
description = "Type-check and build the frontend for production"
run = "npm run build"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:test"]
description = "Run Vitest unit tests"
run = "npm run test"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:check"]
description = "Lint + typecheck + unit tests + production build"
run = "npm run check"
dir = "ui"

[tasks."ui:generate-types"]
description = "Regenerate TypeScript types from the OpenAPI spec"
run = "npm run generate:types"
dir = "ui"

# ---- Docs (the bridge between the JVM and Python worlds) ------------------------

[tasks."docs:deps"]
description = "Install MkDocs deps (uv sync into .venv)"
run = "uv sync"

[tasks."docs:api"]
description = "Generate Kotlin API docs (Dokka) into the site tree"
run = """
./gradlew dokkaHtmlCollector
rm -rf docs/reference/kdoc
cp -r build/dokka/htmlCollector docs/reference/kdoc
"""

[tasks."docs:build"]
description = "Build the static docs site (includes Dokka output)"
depends = ["docs:deps", "docs:api"]
run = "uv run mkdocs build --strict"

[tasks."docs:serve"]
description = "Live-preview the docs site"
depends = ["docs:deps"]
run = "uv run mkdocs serve"

# ---- CI entry point -------------------------------------------------------------

[tasks.ci]
description = "Everything CI runs (sequential to avoid Gradle build-lock contention)"
run = """
./gradlew ktlintCheck detekt
./gradlew build
mise run contracts:install
mise run contracts:build
mise run ui:install
mise run ui:check
mise run docs:build
"""

# Reference: Test Coverage

Coverage is generated by **JaCoCo** and published to [`reference/coverage/`](../coverage/index.html).

To regenerate locally:

```bash
./gradlew build               # run tests (produces .exec files)
mise run docs:coverage        # generates report, copies HTML to docs/reference/coverage/
mise run docs:build           # full site including coverage
```

## What is measured

Line and branch coverage across all Kotlin source modules:

- `domain` — domain model and flow interpreter
- `application` — use-case layer (task actions, queries)
- `adapters/persistence-postgres` — jOOQ repository implementations
- `adapters/rest-api` — Ktor route handlers
- `adapters/temporal` — Temporal workflow/activity adapters
- `adapters/eventing-cloudevents` — CloudEvents mapping

Integration tests (those backed by Testcontainers) are included in the measurement.

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel any in-progress run for the same branch/PR when a new run starts.
# head_ref is the source branch name on PR events; ref_name is the branch on push events.
# Both resolve to the same value for a given branch, so push + PR don't double-run.
concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  ci:
    name: Build, test & docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16-alpine
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: wrkflw
          POSTGRES_USER: wrkflw
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: wrkflw
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install tools (JDK 21 + uv)
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        # Reads mise.toml; installs & caches temurin-21 + uv; trusts config.

      - name: Cache Gradle packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.gradle/caches
            ~/.gradle/wrapper
          key: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle.kts', 'gradle/libs.versions.toml') }}
          restore-keys: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache uv packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/uv
          key: uv-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('uv.lock') }}
          restore-keys: uv-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache npm packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ui/node_modules
            contracts/node_modules
          key: npm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('ui/package-lock.json', 'contracts/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: npm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Run CI (lint + build + docs)
        run: mise run ci

  docs-deploy:
    name: Publish docs to GitHub Pages
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: ci
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    permissions:
      pages: write
      id-token: write
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deploy.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install tools
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2

      - name: Cache Gradle packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.gradle/caches
            ~/.gradle/wrapper
          key: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle.kts', 'gradle/libs.versions.toml') }}
          restore-keys: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache uv packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/uv
          key: uv-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('uv.lock') }}
          restore-keys: uv-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Build docs
        run: mise run docs:build

      - name: Upload Pages artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: site/

      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deploy
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel any in-progress run for the same branch/PR when a new run starts.
# head_ref is the source branch name on PR events; ref_name is the branch on push events.
# Both resolve to the same value for a given branch, so push + PR don't double-run.
concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  ci:
    name: Build, test & docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      checks: write
      contents: read
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16-alpine
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: wrkflw
          POSTGRES_USER: wrkflw
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: wrkflw
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install tools (JDK 21 + uv)
        uses: jdx/mise-action@v2
        # Reads mise.toml; installs & caches temurin-21 + uv; trusts config.

      - name: Cache Gradle packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.gradle/caches
            ~/.gradle/wrapper
          key: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/*.gradle.kts', 'gradle/libs.versions.toml') }}
          restore-keys: gradle-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache uv packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/uv
          key: uv-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('uv.lock') }}
          restore-keys: uv-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Cache npm packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ui/node_modules
            contracts/node_modules
          key: npm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('ui/package-lock.json', 'contracts/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: npm-${{ runner.os }}-

      - name: Run CI (lint + build + docs)
        run: mise run ci

      - name: Publish test results
        uses: dorny/test-reporter@v1
        if: always()
        with:
          name: JUnit Tests
          path: "**/build/test-results/test/*.xml"
          reporter: java-junit

      - name: Coverage summary
        if: always()
        run: |
          python3 - <<'EOF'
          import re, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
          content = open("build/reports/jacoco/root/jacocoRootReport.xml").read()
          content = re.sub(r"<!DOCTYPE[^>]*>", "", content)
          root = ET.fromstring(content)
          rows = []
          for c in root.findall("counter"):
              missed, covered = int(c.get("missed")), int(c.get("covered"))
              total = missed + covered
              pct = f"{covered/total*100:.1f}%" if total > 0 else "n/a"
              rows.append(f"| {c.get('type').capitalize()} | {pct} | {covered}/{total} |")
          summary = "\n".join(rows)
          with open(os.environ["GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"], "a") as f:
              f.write(f"## Test Coverage\n\n| Metric | Coverage | Lines |\n|--------|----------|-------|\n{summary}\n")
          EOF
        env:
          PYTHONPATH: ""

      - name: Upload coverage report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: coverage-report
          path: build/reports/jacoco/root/html/
          retention-days: 30

  docs-deploy:
    name: Publish docs to GitHub Pages
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: ci
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'

      - name: Upload Pages artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: site/

      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deploy

Everything looks good. Here's a summary of all changes made:

---

**`build-logic/src/main/kotlin/testing.gradle.kts`** — added `jacoco` plugin so every module's test run emits a `build/jacoco/test.exec` file automatically.

**`build.gradle.kts`** — added `jacoco` plugin + `jacocoRootReport` task that aggregates all `.exec` files across submodules into a single XML + HTML report at `build/reports/jacoco/root/`.

**`mise.toml`** — added `docs:coverage` task (runs `jacocoRootReport`, copies HTML to `docs/reference/coverage/`) and wired it as a dependency of `docs:build`.

**`docs/reference/coverage.md`** — new reference page explaining coverage and how to regenerate it locally.

**`mkdocs.yml`** — added "Test Coverage" under the Reference nav section.

**`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — three additions after `mise run ci`:
- `dorny/test-reporter@v1` reads the JUnit XML from `**/build/test-results/test/*.xml` and posts a **Checks tab** result on every PR/push
- A Python inline script writes a coverage table (instruction/branch/line %) to the **job summary**
- `actions/upload-artifact@v4` uploads the full JaCoCo HTML report as a downloadable artifact (30-day retention)

**`.gitignore`** — added `docs/reference/coverage/` (generated, mirrors the existing `docs/reference/kdoc/` exclusion).
